Zero-day exploitation gains code execution in the public service.
Persistence follows successful runtime compromise.
Runtime compromise pivots laterally to administrative data paths.
On-premise host is encrypted by attacker payloads.
Object storage is rewritten and effectively encrypted.
Cloud workloads are encrypted under the same detonation path.
Cross-tenant federation path is abused as a privilege bridge.
Rogue service principal extends persistence in the federation plane.
Federated identity assumes privileged control via the trust path.
Forged but valid tokens are minted for privileged identities.
Attacker steals the IdP signing key and becomes the trust anchor.
Forged identity pivots across cloud and SaaS services.
Forged attestation causes shadow workloads to appear trusted.
Node-level compromise reaches the hardware root of trust.
Hardware-backed key material is extracted from the trust anchor.
Data leaves the cloned workload after disk decryption and trust bypass.
Cloned workload impersonates the production database tier.

scenario_mappings.title: Spam Burst / Relay Probing
scenario_mappings.description: Inbound spam burst and relay probing against the monitored tenant edge.

relationships.note: Spam and relay probing pressure the email edge.

scenario_mappings.title: Malware Attachment Attempt
scenario_mappings.description: Malware-laced message delivery targeting user inbox workflows.

relationships.note: Attachment delivery targets inbox execution paths.

scenario_mappings.title: Unauthorized Access / Credential Compromise
scenario_mappings.description: Credential compromise enables unauthorized access and suspicious session start.

relationships.note: Credential stuffing pattern leads to account compromise.

scenario_mappings.title: Business Email Compromise Attempt
scenario_mappings.description: Mailbox takeover and message manipulation drive financial fraud risk.
scenario_mappings.classification: Business Email Compromise (BEC) - Active Account Takeover

relationships.note: Reply-to and mailbox rules are altered during BEC.
Executive identity takeover drives fraud operations.

scenario_mappings.title: OAuth Consent Abuse
scenario_mappings.description: Rogue OAuth application gains privileged delegated access.

relationships.note: Unverified app uses high-risk delegated scopes.
Consent flow grants attacker-controlled token access.

scenario_mappings.title: Vendor Compromise
scenario_mappings.description: Downstream fraud path begins with compromised vendor automation access.

relationships.note: Vendor automation identity seeds downstream compromise.
Vendor access path becomes the attacker ingress route.

scenario_mappings.title: Insider Misuse
scenario_mappings.description: Privileged insider abuse expands access and stages data for exfiltration.
scenario_mappings.classification: Insider Misuse - Privilege Creep and Data Exfiltration

relationships.note: Insider stages data into controlled storage paths.
Insider or coerced privileged access drives data staging.

scenario_mappings.title: Token Replay and Impossible Travel
scenario_mappings.description: Session hijack uses replayed tokens across impossible travel conditions.

relationships.note: Bearer token replay drives the impossible travel signal.
Attacker reuses valid session state to impersonate the user.

scenario_mappings.title: Lateral Movement via OAuth App
scenario_mappings.description: OAuth application abuse impersonates multiple users and pivots across cloud data paths.
scenario_mappings.classification: Lateral Movement via Compromised OAuth App

relationships.note: OAuth token abuse pivots across multiple identities.
OAuth app laterally accesses additional users.
Attacker-controlled app becomes a lateral movement hub.

scenario_mappings.title: Service Principal Hijack
scenario_mappings.description: Compromised service principal seizes production workload permissions.
scenario_mappings.classification: Cloud Workload Compromise - Service Principal Hijack

relationships.note: Service principal compromise unlocks workload actions.
Hijacked service principal operates directly on production workloads.

scenario_mappings.title: Kubernetes Sidecar Breakout
scenario_mappings.description: Container breakout crosses the pod boundary and reaches the underlying node.

relationships.note: Breakout escapes container isolation into the node.
Attacker gains runtime control of the sidecar path.

scenario_mappings.title: CI/CD Pipeline Poisoning
scenario_mappings.description: Malicious artifact drift enters production through poisoned automation.

relationships.note: Poisoned artifact contaminates the release path.
Automation trust is subverted by attacker-controlled artifact flow.

scenario_mappings.title: Zero-Day Runtime Exploit
scenario_mappings.description: Public-facing service compromise expands into runtime control, exfiltration, and persistence.

relationships.note: Zero-day exploitation gains code execution in the public service.

Persistence follows successful runtime compromise.

scenario_mappings.title: Hybrid-Cloud Ransomware
scenario_mappings.description: Cross-environment encryption strikes on-prem systems, cloud VMs, Kubernetes workloads, and storage.

relationships.note: On-premise host is encrypted by attacker payloads.
Cloud workloads are encrypted under the same detonation path.

scenario_mappings.title: Cross-Tenant Federation Abuse
scenario_mappings.description: Federation trust abuse enables privileged cross-tenant takeover.

scenario_mappings.title: Global Identity Provider Compromise
scenario_mappings.description: OIDC signing key theft collapses the trust anchor for all downstream identities.
